```{r custom_gene_heatmap}
##################################################################################################################
#3. The Violin plot with the custom genes either as violin or as heatmap with the same clusters as above
##################################################################################################################

# Note, I am unclear if Nils wants heatmap sep by treatment and cluster or just by cluster. 
# will generate both plots 

# firstly, treatment and cluster sep 

# aggregate expression for heatmap visualisation
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"
pseudo.cd45pos <- AggregateExpression(day4.seurat,
                                      assays = "SCT",
                                      return.seurat = TRUE, 
                                      group.by = c("cell_type", "Treatment"))

# genes to plot provided by Nils
custom.genes <- c("Il1b", "Il6", "Il12a", "Il12b", "Ifng", "Tnf", "Ccl2", "Ccl3", "Ccl4", "Ccl5", "Cxcl1", "Cxcl9", "Cxcl10") # Cxcl13 not present in CD45Pos dataset


###################
# Plot heatmap
###################

DoHeatmap(pseudo.cd45pos, 
          features = custom.genes, 
          draw.lines = FALSE, 
          angle = 90,
          raster = FALSE) + 
  scico::scale_fill_scico(palette = "batlow", 
                          direction = 1, 
                          na.value = "white")

dev.copy(pdf, paste0(output.dir, "Heatmap_pseudobulk_Day_4_only_treatment_cluster_custom_genes.pdf"))
dev.off()




# secondly, cluster sep only

# aggregate expression for heatmap visualisation
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"
pseudo.cd45pos <- AggregateExpression(day4.seurat,
                                      assays = "SCT",
                                      return.seurat = TRUE, 
                                      group.by = c("cell_type"))


###################
# Plot heatmap
###################

DoHeatmap(pseudo.cd45pos, 
          features = custom.genes, 
          draw.lines = FALSE, 
          angle = 90,
          raster = FALSE) + 
  scico::scale_fill_scico(palette = "batlow", 
                          direction = 1, 
                          na.value = "white")

dev.copy(pdf, paste0(output.dir, "Heatmap_pseudobulk_Day_4_only_cluster_custom_genes.pdf"))
dev.off()


```


### Dot plots of select GO terms
```{r Dotplots_GO_terms}

###################################################################################################################################################
#4. Dotplots for GO terms Neutrophils, monocytes, macrophages and DCs 
###################################################################################################################################################

# firstly calculate DEGs treatment-wise - this is redone as this object is just Day 4 and no basophils

if(long.compute){
  
  
  day4.seurat <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(day4.seurat)
  
  # set assay
  DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"
  
  
  
  # add annotation for treatment 
  day4.seurat@meta.data$Treatment_cluster <- paste0(day4.seurat@meta.data$Treatment, "_", day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type)
  
  # set Idents
  Idents(day4.seurat) <- "Treatment_cluster"
  
  
  cluster.ids <- unique(day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type)
  
  # create list to store output
  DEG.list <- list()
  
  # run through DEG specifically between vehicle and treatment for each cluster 
  
  for(i in seq_along(cluster.ids)){
    temp.de <- FindMarkers(day4.seurat,
                           assay = "SCT",
                           ident.1 = paste0("PDS_", cluster.ids[i]),
                           ident.2 = paste0("Vehicle_", cluster.ids[i]),
                           only.pos = FALSE, 
                           recorrect_umi = FALSE,
                           verbose = TRUE)
    
    DEG.list[[i]] <- temp.de
    
    # save to file 
    write.csv(temp.de, paste0(output.dir, "DEGs_PDS_", cluster.ids[i], "_vs_Vehicle_", cluster.ids[i], ".csv"))
    
    
  }
  # add names to DEG.list
  names(DEG.list) <- cluster.ids
  
  # save DEG list to file 
  saveRDS(DEG.list, paste0(output.dir, "DEG_list_object_day4_no_Baso.rds"))
  
  
}else{
  
  DEG.list <- readRDS(paste0(output.dir, "DEG_list_object_day4_no_Baso.rds"))
  
}


########################
# Set up enrichr params
########################

# load library
library("enrichR")
library("clusterProfiler")
library("dplyr")
library("ggplot2")
library("scales")  # For the log transformation and labels

# set variables
enrichR::setEnrichrSite(site = "Enrichr")

dbs <- enrichR::listEnrichrDbs()
dbs <- sort(dbs$libraryName)


# Choose the dataset to query against.
dbs_use <- c("GO_Biological_Process_2021", "KEGG_2019_Mouse")


##############
# Functions 
##############
# Define the function
filter_terms <- function(data, go_terms, group_name, prefix = "") {
  # Perform the filtering and mutation
  result <- data %>%
    dplyr::select(c(Term, Overlap, Adjusted.P.value, Odds.Ratio, Combined.Score)) %>%
    filter(grepl(paste(go_terms, collapse = "|"), Term, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
    mutate(Term = paste0(prefix, Term), 
           group = group_name)
  
  print(result)
  return(result)
}


process_data <- function(input.1, input.2, input.3, input.4, pval_threshold = 0.05){
  
  # join up and down term tables together
  combined.term.table <- bind_rows(input.1, input.2, input.3, input.4)
  
  # additional formatting of dataframe 
  combined.term.table <- combined.term.table %>%
    mutate(Overlap_Ratio = as.numeric(sub("/.*", "", Overlap)) / as.numeric(sub(".*/", "", Overlap))) %>% # Create overlap ratio
    mutate(color_value = ifelse(Adjusted.P.value > pval_threshold, NA, Combined.Score)) %>% # create new column so that in.sig values are greyed out
    mutate(color_value_log = log1p(color_value)) # Using log1p to handle zero values
  
  # Reorder the 'group' factor levels
  combined.term.table$group <- factor(combined.term.table$group, levels = c("PDS", "Vehicle"))
  
  # Order dataframe by group and combined score descending
  combined.term.table <- combined.term.table %>%
    arrange(group, desc(Combined.Score)) %>% 
    mutate(Term_order = factor(Term, levels = unique(Term)))
  
  return(combined.term.table)
  
}

plot_all <- function(term.table, title.var = " Ontology Terms", save.path){
  
  p1 <- ggplot(term.table, aes(x = group, y = Term_order, size = Overlap_Ratio)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = color_value_log), na.rm = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", na.value = "gray",
                         name = "Combined Score",
                         labels = scales::label_log()) +  # Apply log scale labels
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(0, 10)) +  # Adjust range to enhance size difference
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +  # Reverse the order of the y-axis
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title.var, x = "Treatment group (Day 4)", y = NULL)
  print(p1)
  ggsave(save.path, p1)
  
}

plot_pds_sig <- function(term.table, title.var = "", pval_threshold = 0.05, save.path){
  
  
  # Filter significant pathways in the PDS group
  significant_pds_terms <- term.table %>%
    filter(group == "PDS" & Adjusted.P.value < pval_threshold) %>%
    pull(Term)  # Extract terms as a vector
  
  # Filter the combined table to keep significant terms in PDS and all terms in Vehicle group
  filtered_combined_table <- term.table %>%
    filter(Term %in% significant_pds_terms)
  
  
  p1 <- ggplot(filtered_combined_table, aes(x = group, y = Term_order, size = Overlap_Ratio)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = color_value_log), na.rm = TRUE) +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", na.value = "gray",
                         name = "Combined Score",
                         labels = scales::label_log()) +  # Apply log scale labels
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(0, 10)) +  # Adjust range to enhance size difference
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +  # Reverse the order of the y-axis
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title.var, x = "Treatment group (Day 4)", y = NULL)
  
  print(p1)
  ggsave(save.path, p1)
  
}
##################
#a. Neutrophils: 
##################

# subset seurat object for cell_type in question
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"

Idents(day4.seurat) <- day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type
Neutrophil.seurat <- subset(day4.seurat, idents = "Neutrophils")
table(Neutrophil.seurat@meta.data$Treatment)
Idents(Neutrophil.seurat) <- Neutrophil.seurat@meta.data$Treatment

# calculate enrichment

# filter for upregulated genes
up.genes <- DEG.list$Neutrophils %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>% 
  rownames()

dn.genes <- DEG.list$Neutrophils %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>% 
  rownames()


# Retrieve the enriched terms.
up.enriched.terms <- enrichR::enrichr(up.genes, dbs_use)
dn.enriched.terms <- enrichR::enrichr(dn.genes, dbs_use)

# Filter GO output for plotting and based on terms Nils would like to highlight

# Neutrophil GO-BP terms 
GO.terms <- c("cytokine-mediated signaling pathway", "neutrophil degranulation", "neutrophil mediated immunity", "cellular response to type I interferon", 
                         "phagosome acidification", "phagosome maturation", "translation", "gene expression", "ribosome biogenesis")
GO.terms <- paste0("^", GO.terms, " ")

length(GO.terms)

PDS.go <- filter_terms(up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021,
                       go_terms = GO.terms,
                       group_name = "PDS")

Vehcile.go <- filter_terms(dn.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021,
                           go_terms = GO.terms,
                           group_name = "Vehicle")

# Neutrophil KEGG terms 
KEGG.terms <- c("Lysosome", "Phagosome", "NOD-like receptor signaling pathway", "Apoptosis", "NF-kappa B signaling pathway", "TNF signaling pathway",
                "Antigen processing and presentation", "RIG-I-like receptor signaling pathway", "Ribosome", "Oxidative phosphorylation")
KEGG.terms <- paste0("^", KEGG.terms, "$")
length(KEGG.terms)

PDS.kegg <- filter_terms(up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse,
                         go_terms = KEGG.terms,
                         group_name = "PDS", 
                         prefix = "KEGG: ")

Vehcile.kegg <- filter_terms(dn.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse,
                             go_terms = KEGG.terms,
                             group_name = "Vehicle", 
                             prefix = "KEGG: ")

term.table.out <- process_data(PDS.go, PDS.kegg, Vehcile.go, Vehcile.kegg)

plot_all(term.table = term.table.out, save.path = paste0(output.dir, "test.pdf"))

plot_pds_sig(term.table = term.table.out, save.path = paste0(output.dir, "test_sig.pdf"))



# combine all data

# join up and down term tables together
combined.term.table <- bind_rows(PDS.go, PDS.kegg, Vehcile.go, Vehcile.kegg)

# Set the p-value threshold
pval_threshold <- 0.05


# additional formatting of dataframe 
combined.term.table <- combined.term.table %>%
  mutate(Overlap_Ratio = as.numeric(sub("/.*", "", Overlap)) / as.numeric(sub(".*/", "", Overlap))) %>% # Create overlap ratio
  mutate(color_value = ifelse(Adjusted.P.value > pval_threshold, NA, Combined.Score)) %>% # create new column so that in.sig values are greyed out
  mutate(color_value_log = log1p(color_value)) # Using log1p to handle zero values
  
# Reorder the 'group' factor levels
combined.term.table$group <- factor(combined.term.table$group, levels = c("PDS", "Vehicle"))

# Order dataframe by group and combined score descending
combined.term.table <- combined.term.table %>%
  arrange(group, desc(Combined.Score)) %>% 
  mutate(Term_order = factor(Term, levels = unique(Term)))


# Plot with the custom color scale on a log scale
ggplot(combined.term.table, aes(x = group, y = Term_order, size = Overlap_Ratio)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = color_value_log), na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", na.value = "gray",
                       name = "Combined Score",
                       labels = scales::label_log()) +  # Apply log scale labels
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(0, 10)) +  # Adjust range to enhance size difference
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +  # Reverse the order of the y-axis
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Neutrophil Ontology terms", x = "Treatment group (Day 4)", y = NULL)










# Calculate the min and max adjusted p-values below the threshold
#pval_min <- min(combined.term.table$color_value, na.rm = TRUE)
#pval_max <- max(combined.term.table$color_value, na.rm = TRUE)

```



### Heatmaps highlighting genes in specific genesets
```{r Heatmaps_plots_highlighting_specific_genesets}

###################################################################################################################################################
#4. Volcano plots for Neutrophils, monocytes, macrophages and DCs highlighting and labeling top regulated genes with the following gene sets:
###################################################################################################################################################

# firstly calculate DEGs treatment-wise - this is redone as this object is just Day 4 and no basophils

if(long.compute){
  
  
  day4.seurat <- PrepSCTFindMarkers(day4.seurat)
  
  # set assay
  DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"
  
  
  
  # add annotation for treatment 
  day4.seurat@meta.data$Treatment_cluster <- paste0(day4.seurat@meta.data$Treatment, "_", day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type)
  
  # set Idents
  Idents(day4.seurat) <- "Treatment_cluster"
  
  
  cluster.ids <- unique(day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type)
  
  # create list to store output
  DEG.list <- list()
  
  # run through DEG specifically between vehicle and treatment for each cluster 
  
  for(i in seq_along(cluster.ids)){
    temp.de <- FindMarkers(day4.seurat,
                           assay = "SCT",
                           ident.1 = paste0("PDS_", cluster.ids[i]),
                           ident.2 = paste0("Vehicle_", cluster.ids[i]),
                           only.pos = FALSE, 
                           recorrect_umi = FALSE,
                           verbose = TRUE)
    
    DEG.list[[i]] <- temp.de
    
    # save to file 
    write.csv(temp.de, paste0(output.dir, "DEGs_PDS_", cluster.ids[i], "_vs_Vehicle_", cluster.ids[i], ".csv"))
    
    
  }
  # add names to DEG.list
  names(DEG.list) <- cluster.ids
  
  # save DEG list to file 
  saveRDS(DEG.list, paste0(output.dir, "DEG_list_object_day4_no_Baso.rds"))
  
  
}else{
  
  DEG.list <- readRDS(paste0(output.dir, "DEG_list_object_day4_no_Baso.rds"))
  
}


########################
# Set up enrichr params
########################

# load library
library(enrichR)

# set variables
enrichR::setEnrichrSite(site = "Enrichr")

dbs <- enrichR::listEnrichrDbs()
dbs <- sort(dbs$libraryName)


# Choose the dataset to query against.
dbs_use <- c("GO_Biological_Process_2021", "KEGG_2019_Mouse", "GO_Molecular_Function_2023")

############################################################
# Define function for downstream filtering and plotting
############################################################
go.heatmap <- function(input.data = NULL, GO.genes = NULL, celltype.name = "TEST", GO.name = "TEST"){
  
  # remove low expressed genes
  goi <- input.data@assays$SCT@data %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column() %>%
    dplyr::filter(rowname %in% GO.genes) %>%
    dplyr::rowwise() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(counts = sum(c_across(-rowname))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(counts > 100) %>%
    dplyr::pull(rowname)
  
  # scale data
  input.data <- ScaleData(input.data, features = goi)
  
  # plot heatmap
  p1 <- DoHeatmap(input.data, 
                  features = goi, 
                  draw.lines = T, 
                  angle = 90,
                  group.by = "Treatment",
                  raster = FALSE) + 
    ggtitle(GO.name) +
    scico::scale_fill_scico(palette = "batlow", 
                            direction = 1, 
                            na.value = "white") 
  ggsave(paste0(output.dir, "Heatmap_Day4_", celltype.name, "_only_group_by_treatment_", GO.name, ".pdf"), plot = p1)
  
}

##################
#a. Neutrophils: 
##################
#neutrophil degranulation (GO:0043312), neutrophil mediated immunity (GO:0002446...), Phagosome+Lysosome (KEGG)

# subset seurat object for cell_type in question
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"

Idents(day4.seurat) <- day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type
Neutrophil.seurat <- subset(day4.seurat, idents = "Neutrophils")
table(Neutrophil.seurat@meta.data$Treatment)
Idents(Neutrophil.seurat) <- Neutrophil.seurat@meta.data$Treatment

#Neutrophil.seurat <- subset(Neutrophil.seurat, downsample = 500)


########################
# calculate enrichment
########################

# filter for upregulated genes
sig.up <- DEG.list$Neutrophils %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 0) 

# List of genes to use as input.
up.genes <- unique(rownames(sig.up))

# Retrieve the enriched terms.
up.enriched.terms <- enrichR::enrichr(up.genes, dbs_use)

term.table <- up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::select(c(Term, Overlap, Adjusted.P.value, Odds.Ratio))



################################
# GO Biological process terms 
################################

# Note that the genes in degranulation and neutrophil mediated immunity gene sets are identical. this is not an error just a reflection of the overlap in these genesets

# show upregulated GO Biological process terms
up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# Degranulation - GO:0043312
GO.Degranulation <- up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("GO:0043312", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

GO.Degranulation <- stringr::str_to_title(GO.Degranulation)



library("clusterProfiler")


dotplot(ego, showCategory=30) + ggtitle("dotplot for ORA")
# Simulated data example
df <- data.frame(
  Description = c("T cell activation", "JAK-STAT cascade", "Cytokine production", 
                  "Regulation of cell killing", "STAT cascade", "Cellular response to lipid", 
                  "Protein folding", "Negative regulation of multi-organism process"),
  GeneRatio = c(0.12, 0.08, 0.15, 0.10, 0.07, 0.09, 0.11, 0.06),
  p.adjust = c(0.02, 0.05, 0.01, 0.03, 0.04, 0.05, 0.02, 0.01),
  Count = c(25, 40, 30, 20, 10, 15, 18, 12),
  group = rep(c("Cbl", "WT"), each = 4)
)

# Plotting
ggplot(df, aes(x = group, y = reorder(Description, -GeneRatio), size = GeneRatio, color = p.adjust)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Biological Process", x = "Group", y = NULL)





head(ego)
head(ggo)
# Entrez gene ID
head(gene)
edo2 <- gseGO(up.genes, ont = "BP", OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db)
dotplot(edo, showCategory=30) + ggtitle("dotplot for ORA")
dotplot(edo2, showCategory=30) + ggtitle("dotplot for GSEA")


?SCpubr::do_DotPlot()


DotPlot_scCustom(Neutrophil.seurat, features = GO.Degranulation, flip_axes = T)



# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Neutrophil.seurat, 
           GO.genes = GO.Degranulation, 
           celltype.name = "Neutrophils", 
           GO.name = "Degranulation_0043312")


# Neutrophil mediated immunity - GO:0002446
GO.Neut.immunity <- up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("GO:0002446", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

GO.Neut.immunity <- stringr::str_to_title(GO.Neut.immunity)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Neutrophil.seurat, 
           GO.genes = GO.Neut.immunity, 
           celltype.name = "Neutrophils", 
           GO.name = "Neut_Immunity_0002446")



################################
# KEGG pathways
################################

# show upregulated Kegg terms
up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# Lysosome and Phagosome (KEGG)
KEGG.Phago.lyso <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl(paste0(c("Lysosome", "Phagosome"), collapse = "|"), Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.Phago.lyso <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.Phago.lyso)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Neutrophil.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.Phago.lyso, 
           celltype.name = "Neutrophils", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_Lyso_Phagosome")

rm(Neutrophil.seurat)


##################
#b. Monocytes: 
##################
#positive regulation of response to exter.. (GO), positive regulation of inflammatory response (GO), 
#Phagosome (KEGG), Toll-like receptor signaling pathway (KEGG)

# subset seurat object for cell_type in question
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"

Idents(day4.seurat) <- day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type
Monocytes.seurat <- subset(day4.seurat, idents = "Monocytes")
table(Monocytes.seurat@meta.data$Treatment)
Idents(Monocytes.seurat) <- Monocytes.seurat@meta.data$Treatment

Monocytes.seurat <- subset(Monocytes.seurat, downsample = 500)


########################
# calculate enrichment
########################

# filter for upregulated genes
sig.up <- DEG.list$Monocytes %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 0) 

# List of genes to use as input.
up.genes <- unique(rownames(sig.up))

# Retrieve the enriched terms.
up.enriched.terms <- enrichR::enrichr(up.genes, dbs_use)


################################
# GO Biological process terms 
################################

# show upregulated GO Biological process terms
up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# positive regulation of response to external stimulus (GO:0032103)
GO.external.stim <- up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("GO:0032103", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

GO.external.stim <- stringr::str_to_title(GO.external.stim)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Monocytes.seurat, 
           GO.genes = GO.external.stim, 
           celltype.name = "Monocytes", 
           GO.name = "Pos_reg_external_stim_0032103")

################################
# KEGG pathways
################################

# show upregulated Kegg terms
up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# Phagosome (KEGG)
KEGG.Phago <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("Phagosome", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.Phago <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.Phago)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Monocytes.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.Phago, 
           celltype.name = "Monocytes", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_Phagosome")


# Toll-like receptor signaling pathway (KEGG)
KEGG.TLR <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("Toll-like receptor signaling pathway", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.TLR <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.TLR)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Monocytes.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.TLR, 
           celltype.name = "Monocytes", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_TLR")

rm(Monocytes.seurat)

####################
#c. Macrophages: 
####################
#pattern-recognition receptor signaling (GO),
#Toll-like receptor signaling pathway (KEGG), Antigen processing and presentation (KEGG), Phagosome (KEGG)


# subset seurat object for cell_type in question
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"

Idents(day4.seurat) <- day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type
Macrophages.seurat <- subset(day4.seurat, idents = "Macrophages")
table(Macrophages.seurat@meta.data$Treatment)
Idents(Macrophages.seurat) <- Macrophages.seurat@meta.data$Treatment

Macrophages.seurat <- subset(Macrophages.seurat, downsample = 500)


########################
# calculate enrichment
########################

# filter for upregulated genes
sig.up <- DEG.list$Macrophages %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 0) 

# List of genes to use as input.
up.genes <- unique(rownames(sig.up))

# Retrieve the enriched terms.
up.enriched.terms <- enrichR::enrichr(up.genes, dbs_use)


################################
# GO Biological process terms 
################################

# show upregulated GO Biological process terms
up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# pattern recognition receptor signaling pathway (GO:0002221)
GO.pattern <- up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("GO:0002221", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

GO.pattern <- stringr::str_to_title(GO.pattern)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Macrophages.seurat, 
           GO.genes = GO.pattern, 
           celltype.name = "Macrophages", 
           GO.name = "PRR_signaling_pathway_0002221")

################################
# KEGG pathways
################################

# show upregulated Kegg terms
up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# Toll-like receptor signaling pathway (KEGG)
KEGG.TLR <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("Toll-like receptor signaling pathway", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.TLR <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.TLR)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Macrophages.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.TLR, 
           celltype.name = "Macrophages", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_TLR")

# Phagosome (KEGG)
KEGG.Phago <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("Phagosome", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.Phago <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.Phago)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Macrophages.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.Phago, 
           celltype.name = "Macrophages", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_Phagosome")



# Antigen processing and presentation (KEGG)
KEGG.APP <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("Antigen processing and presentation", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.APP <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.APP)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = Macrophages.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.APP, 
           celltype.name = "Macrophages", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_Antigen_Processing")

###########
#d. DCs: 
###########
# inflammatory response (GO:0006954)
#Toll-like receptor signaling pathway (KEGG), 

# subset seurat object for cell_type in question
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"

Idents(day4.seurat) <- day4.seurat@meta.data$cell_type
DC.seurat <- subset(day4.seurat, idents = "DC")
table(DC.seurat@meta.data$Treatment)
Idents(DC.seurat) <- DC.seurat@meta.data$Treatment

DC.seurat <- subset(DC.seurat, downsample = 300)


########################
# calculate enrichment
########################

# filter for upregulated genes
sig.up <- DEG.list$DC %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 0) 

# List of genes to use as input.
up.genes <- unique(rownames(sig.up))

# Retrieve the enriched terms.
up.enriched.terms <- enrichR::enrichr(up.genes, dbs_use)


################################
# GO Biological process terms 
################################

# show upregulated GO Biological process terms
up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# inflammatory response (GO:0006954)
GO.inflam <- up.enriched.terms$GO_Biological_Process_2021 %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("GO:0006954", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

GO.inflam <- stringr::str_to_title(GO.inflam)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = DC.seurat, 
           GO.genes = GO.inflam, 
           celltype.name = "DC", 
           GO.name = "Inflam_0006954")

################################
# KEGG pathways
################################

# show upregulated Kegg terms
up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  pull(Term)

# Toll-like receptor signaling pathway (KEGG)
KEGG.TLR <- up.enriched.terms$KEGG_2019_Mouse %>%
  dplyr::filter(Adjusted.P.value < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(grepl("Toll-like receptor signaling pathway", Term)) %>%
  pull(Genes) %>%
  strsplit(";") %>%
  unlist()

KEGG.TLR <- stringr::str_to_title(KEGG.TLR)

# Filter and plot 
go.heatmap(input.data = DC.seurat, 
           GO.genes = KEGG.TLR, 
           celltype.name = "DC", 
           GO.name = "KEGG_TLR")











FDR.theshold <- 0.01
logFC.threshold <- 1

# filter data
input.data <- DEG.list$Macrophages %>%
  dplyr::rename(logFC = avg_log2FC) %>%
  dplyr::rename(FDR = p_val_adj) %>%
  filter(FDR < FDR.theshold)

# color points
volcano.col <- usefulfunctions::colour.points.volcano(input.data,
                                                      increase.col = "Red",
                                                      decreased.col = "Blue",
                                                      FDR.cutoff = FDR.theshold, 
                                                      logFC.cutoff = logFC.threshold)
# plot heatmap
   
      usefulfunctions::custom.enhanced.volcano(input.data, 
                                               selectLab = KEGG.APP,
                                               pCutoff = FDR.theshold,
                                               FCcutoff = logFC.threshold,
                                               title = paste0(names(DEG.list)[i], " PDS vs Vehicle"),
                                               subtitle = paste0("adjPval threshold = ", FDR.theshold, " & log2FC threshold = ", logFC.threshold),
                                               colCustom = volcano.col)

















# firstly, extract vectors of genes in the above requested pathways 
custom.genes <- c("Il1b", "Il6", "Il12a", "Il12b", "Ifng", "Tnf", "Ccl2", "Ccl3", "Ccl4", "Ccl5", "Cxcl1", "Cxcl9", "Cxcl10") # Cxcl13 not present in CD45Pos dataset






















```



#The Volcanos should show nicely that the immune cells are activated and mediating immunity after PDS treatment. If the "custom genes" (e.g. CCL3, CCL4, Il-12, IL-6, Il-1b,etc.) are not already in the GOs would be cool if you could highlight them as well to show that PDS treatment induces the production of those.


#Additionally it would be cool if you could dive into the deeper analysis of the T cells on day 9 and see if there are any differences in smaller subsets in either numbers or expression between PDS and Vehicle. Will probably not make the cut into this paper, but in case reviewers are asking about the T cells or ortherwise we're planning another paper on PDS and T cells for which that would be great.















## Imputation {.tabset}

### Imputation calculation

```{r seurat_imputation, eval = FALSE}

output.dir <- "results/imputation/"

# Create output directory
if(!dir.exists(paste0(output.dir))){
  dir.create(paste0(output.dir), 
             recursive = T)
}

######################
# Impute values 
######################
if(long.compute){
  day4.seurat <- RunALRA(day4.seurat, 
                             genes.use = rownames(day4.seurat))

  
  # save object 
  SaveSeuratRds(day4.seurat, "saves/CD45Pos_Day4_only_imputed.rds")
  
  }

# 0.00% of the values became negative in the scaling process and were set to zero
# The matrix went from 5.72% nonzero to 27.74% nonzero


####################
# Calculate k value
####################
if(long.compute){
  
  Idents(day4.seurat) <- day4.seurat@meta.data$seurat_clusters
  day4.seurat.small <- subset(day4.seurat, downsample = 100)
  
  ALRA.out <- RunALRA(day4.seurat.small,
                      k.only = TRUE)
  
  ggouts <- ALRAChooseKPlot(ALRA.out)
  
  ggouts
  dev.copy(pdf, paste0(output.dir, "Imputation_K_plot.pdf"))
  dev.off()
  
}


# set default assay back to RNA
DefaultAssay(day4.seurat) <- "SCT"

```
